app.definition: A program that lives on your phone. Example: WhatsApp is an app.

boton.definition: A box on the screen that does something when you tap it.

chatgpt.definition: An AI app that helps you write text. It's free.

canva.definition: An app for creating beautiful images and designs. Has a free version.

copiar.definition: Save text to use elsewhere. Tap and hold, then choose "Copy".

descargar.definition: Save something from the internet to your phone.

ia.definition: Artificial Intelligence. Very smart computer programs that can create text, images, and more.

imagenGenerada.definition: An image created by AI, not a real photo taken with a camera.

internet.definition: The network that connects computers and phones worldwide. You need internet to use ChatGPT and Canva.

pegar.definition: Put the text you copied in a new place. Tap and hold where you want to paste.

prompt.definition: The message you write to AI to ask for something. Like when you ask someone for something via WhatsApp.

redesSociales.definition: Apps where you share photos and messages with others. Example: WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook.

tono.definition: How a text "sounds". It can be friendly, professional, fun, etc.

notifications.skipPresession: Perfect! Let's go straight to the workshop
notifications.presessionComplete: Great! You're ready for the workshop 🎉

experience.nunca: Perfect! This workshop is made for you. Let's go step by step.
experience.poco: Great, today you'll go from theory to practice.
experience.algo: Awesome, you'll learn tricks to improve your results.
experience.mucho: Excellent! You'll discover new ways to use AI.

notifications.copyFailed: Could not copy. Select the text manually.
notifications.fillBusiness: Please write what you sell or do
notifications.emailSuccess: Thanks! We'll let you know soon 💌
notifications.emailError: There was an error. Please try again.

module1.title: What is Artificial Intelligence?
module1.subtitle: Explained simply

outcomes.item1: Explain what AI is in your own words
outcomes.item2: Know what AI can and CANNOT do

module1.completion: Now you understand what AI is and how it can help your business.

explanation.title: AI is like a very smart assistant
explanation.text: Imagine you have an assistant who:
explanation.conclusion: That's AI. You can ask for help and it responds.

canDo.title: What AI CAN do
canDo.item1: Write texts for your social media
canDo.item2: Give you ideas for your business

item1.title: It doesn't really think.
item1.description: It only imitates what it learned.

quiz.title: Activity: True or False?
quiz.instruction: Let's see if you understood. Answer each question:
quiz.explanation1: AI doesn't really think. It only imitates patterns it learned.
quiz.explanation2: Yes, it can help you write. That's one of its best features.
quiz.explanation3: AI can make mistakes. You should always review what it gives you.

ethics.title: Moment of reflection
ethics.text: AI learns from data that humans created. If that data has errors or biases, the AI will have them too.
ethics.question: How do you think this could affect your business?
ethics.shareText: How do you think AI errors could affect a small business? 🤔

successCriteria.introTitle: By the end of this module you will be able to:

module2.title: How to talk to AI
module2.subtitle: Learn to ask for what you need

outcomes.item1: Write a clear message to AI so it helps you
outcomes.item2: Know the free tools you can use today

module2.completion: Now you know how to talk to AI to get good results.

whatIsPrompt.title: What is a "prompt"?
whatIsPrompt.text1: A prompt is the message you write to the AI.
whatIsPrompt.text2: It's like when you ask someone for something on WhatsApp.
whatIsPrompt.exampleText: "Give me 3 ideas to post on Instagram about my soap business"

goldenRule.title: The golden rule
goldenRule.text: The clearer you ask things to the AI, the better it will help you.
goldenRule.goodExample: "Give me 3 Instagram post ideas about natural soaps, with a friendly tone"

method.title: The 4-step method
method.intro: To write a good message to AI, follow these steps:

step1.title: Who are you?
step1.description: Tell it about yourself and your business

step2.title: What do you need?
step2.description: Tell it exactly what you want

step3.title: How do you want it?
step3.description: Describe how the result should look

maria.title: Complete Example: María and her Soaps
maria.subtitle: Let's see how María uses AI for her business

step1.title: Meet María
step1.text3: She sells through WhatsApp and at local fairs.
step1.problem: 😰 Her problem: She doesn't know what to post on her WhatsApp status.

step2.title: María thinks about what she needs
step2.need: What does she need? → Texts for WhatsApp
step2.how: How does she want it? → Short with emojis

step3.title: María writes her message to AI
step3.label: The complete message:

step4.title: AI responds
step4.label: 🤖 AI gave her these options:
step4.option1Text: "Have you tried our oatmeal soaps? Your skin will thank you 🥰 Write me!"
step4.option2Text: "Natural ingredients + lots of love = soaps that care for your skin 💚 Want to know more?"
step4.option3Text: "Perfect little gift for someone special: handmade soap made with love 💝 Ask me!"

result.title: María now has content to post!
result.note: Before, María didn't know what to write. Now she has several options to choose from!

maria.prompt:
I'm María, I have a handmade natural soap business in Bogotá.

I need 3 short texts to post on my WhatsApp status.

Make them short, with emojis, and invite people to ask via WhatsApp.

Use a friendly and warm tone, like talking to a friend.

yourTurn.title: Now it's your turn!
yourTurn.intro: Let's create your first message for AI:
yourTurn.step1Label: What do you sell or do?
yourTurn.step1Placeholder: Example: homemade cookies, clothing, cleaning services...
yourTurn.step3Label: What tone do you want? (choose one)
yourTurn.nextStepHint: 💡 Next step: Copy this message and paste it in ChatGPT or Claude to see what AI responds.

chatgpt.title: ChatGPT
chatgpt.what: What is it? An AI that chats with you and helps you write.
chatgpt.use2: 💡 Give you ideas for your business
chatgpt.step2: Create a free account (with your email)
chatgpt.step3: Write your message and send it

canva.title: Canva
canva.what: What is it? An app to create beautiful designs and images.
canva.step1: Go to canva.com or download the app from your store
canva.step3: Choose a template and customize it

tip.title: Important tip
tip.text: Start with ChatGPT for texts and Canva for images. They're free and easy to use. You don't need more for now!

ethics.title: Moment of reflection
ethics.text: When you give information to AI, that data can be used to train new models. That's why it's important not to share sensitive information about your business or clients.
ethics.question: What information about your business would you NOT give to AI?
ethics.shareText: What information about your business would you NOT give to AI? 🔒

module3.title: Practical Marketing with AI
module3.subtitle: Create real content for your business
module3.completion: Done! You have a piece of marketing written in your voice and tied to a concrete goal for your week.

outcomes.item1: Identify a concrete marketing goal for this week
outcomes.item2: Write a CTO marketing prompt with your audience and your voice
outcomes.item3: Review the AI's response and edit it so it sounds like you
outcomes.item4: Produce a piece of marketing ready to publish today

section1.title: First, decide what you want to achieve

explanation.p1: Before you ask AI for anything, there's something you have to decide: what do you want to achieve this week with your business? AI can write you a thousand posts, but if you're not clear about what they're for, none of them will help.
explanation.p2: Marketing isn't posting for the sake of posting. Marketing is communicating with your customers with a purpose. And the purposes of a small business tend to be few and very concrete:
explanation.listItem1: You want customers who haven't bought in a while to come back.
explanation.listItem2: You want to attract new customers in your area.
explanation.listItem3: You want to sell more of a specific product you have in excess.
explanation.listItem4: You want to announce something new (a flavor, an hour, a promotion).
explanation.listItem5: You want people to remember you exist this week.
explanation.p3: Each of these goals calls for a different kind of message. Inviting a regular who already knows you to try your new yuca bread is not the same as convincing someone walking by to step into your hair salon for the first time.
explanation.p4: That's why the first step is always to choose a goal. Just one. The one for this week.
explanation.sofia: Sofía has a small hair salon in Lima. She runs it alone. Tuesday mornings are always empty. Her goal isn't "more clients in general" — that's too vague. Her concrete goal is: fill three Tuesday morning slots by offering a discount to her regulars.
explanation.conclusion: With a goal that specific, everything that follows gets much easier.

activity.title: Choose your goal for this week
activity.intro: Tap one goal. Just one. The one for this week.

goals.returningClients: 🔁 Get my regulars to come back

activity.detailPlaceholder: Ex: Tuesday mornings, the new yuca bread, the regulars in the neighborhood...
activity.savedLabel: ✅ Goal saved. You'll use it in Section 2.

goalPhrases.returningClients: I want my regulars to come back
goalPhrases.newClients: I want to attract new customers in my area
goalPhrases.sellProduct: I want to sell more of a specific product I have in excess
goalPhrases.announceNew: I want to announce something new
goalPhrases.beRemembered: I want my customers to remember I exist this week

quiz.title: Activity: True or False?
quiz.statement: "Posting on social media every day is a marketing goal."
quiz.correct: Correct! Posting without a goal is noise. Deciding what you want to achieve is what makes every post useful.
quiz.incorrectExplanation: Posting is an activity, not a goal. A goal answers "what is the posting for?" — for example, bringing in new customers or selling more of a specific product. Without a goal, posts don't lead anywhere.

section2.title: Turn your goal into a prompt with CTO

explanation.intro1: You have your goal. Now you're going to ask AI to help you reach it. To do that, you use the CTO method: Context, Task, Output.
explanation.intro2: If you already went through the bonus prompt-writing module, you know this. If not, don't worry — they're three easy letters that help you put together any message to AI. Context is what AI needs to know about you and your business. Task is exactly what you want AI to do. Output is how you want the response to look. With those three parts, any prompt works much better.
explanation.transition: Now, marketing has a particularity: when you talk about your business in general, the context can be short ("I have a bakery in Bogotá"). When you ask AI to write marketing for you, the context needs two more things:
explanation.audience: Who you're talking to. You don't talk to "everyone." You talk to your regulars who already know you, or the moms in the neighborhood who pass by in the morning, or the office workers who stop for coffee before work. Saying who you're talking to completely changes what AI writes for you.
explanation.voice: What's your voice. Your business has a voice. If you talk to your customers like a friend, you don't want text that sounds corporate. If your brand is elegant and polished, you don't want emojis everywhere. Telling AI how you want to sound stops it from giving you something that sounds like it was written by someone else.
explanation.sofiaIntro: Back to Sofía. Her goal is to fill three Tuesday morning slots by offering a discount to her regulars. Her CTO prompt looks like this:
explanation.sofiaContext: I have a small hair salon in Lima. I run it alone. My clients are women between 30 and 55 who live nearby and come every month or two. I talk to them warmly, like a friend, not formally.
explanation.sofiaTask: I need a WhatsApp message to send to my regulars, inviting them to come on a Tuesday morning with 20% off. Only valid for Tuesdays this month.
explanation.sofiaOutput: Keep it short (max 4 lines), warm, with one or two emojis, and end with a question to invite replies.
explanation.conclusion: The same format works for any marketing task. You change the task and the output depending on what you need this week: an Instagram post, a story caption, flyer text, a message for your WhatsApp list.

cto.title: 🎯 Quick reminder: the CTO method
cto.cDesc: Who you are, your business, who you're talking to
cto.tDesc: What you want AI to do
cto.oDesc: How you want the response (tone, length, format)

activity.title: Build your CTO marketing prompt
activity.intro: You've chosen your goal. Now let's fill in the three blocks of the prompt.
activity.businessLabel: What do you sell or do?
activity.businessPlaceholder: Ex: a small hair salon in Lima, an office coffee shop, a neighborhood bakery...
activity.audiencePlaceholder: Ex: my regulars in the neighborhood, young moms, office workers...

voicePhrases.warm: warm and personal, as if talking to a friend

formatPhrases.story: a Facebook or Instagram story caption

activity.outputPlaceholder: Ex: max 3 lines, with emojis, ending in a question
activity.promptContextTemplate: I have a business — {business}. My customers are {audience}. I want you to talk to me in a tone that is {voicePhrase}.
activity.promptOutputDefault: clear, short, and with a call to action
activity.fillAll: First choose a goal in Section 1, then fill in context, audience, voice, and format
activity.savedLabel: ✅ Prompt saved. You'll use it in the final task.

quiz.title: Check what you learned
quiz.question: Which of these prompts is better built for marketing?
quiz.optionA: "Write me something nice for my coffee shop."
quiz.optionB: "I have a small coffee shop in Buenos Aires. My customers are office workers who stop in the morning before work. I need an Instagram post announcing a new coffee from Colombia. Keep it short, with one emoji, in a fresh and friendly tone."
quiz.correct: Exactly! B includes context (what kind of business, where, who it talks to), a specific task (an Instagram post announcing a new coffee), and output (short, one emoji, fresh and friendly tone).
quiz.incorrectExplanation: B works better because AI doesn't have to guess anything. A is vague: AI doesn't know what kind of "something nice" to make, for whom, or in what tone.

explanation.intro1: AI is going to give you something. It's almost never perfect, and it almost always can be improved. Your job doesn't end when AI responds — it starts there. You know your business, your neighborhood, your customers. AI doesn't.
explanation.intro2: Before copying and publishing what AI gave you, run it through these three questions:
explanation.q1Text: Read it under your breath. Is that how you talk? Or does it sound like a TV ad, like a corporation, like someone who isn't you? If it doesn't sound like you, change it. Replace the words you'd never use. Shorten the phrases that felt forced. Your voice matters because it's what your customers recognize and why they choose you.
explanation.q2Title: Would I really talk to my customer this way?
explanation.q2Text: Imagine your most loyal customer reading that message. Is she going to care? Is it going to sound genuine, or like advertising? AI tends to write things that sound like every business at once. Your marketing has to sound like yours — specific, like your neighborhood.
explanation.q3Text: Go back to the goal you chose in Section 1. If your goal was to fill three Tuesday morning slots, the message has to make it clear it's for Tuesday morning. If AI wrote something generic like "come whenever you want," it doesn't meet the goal. Ask AI to adjust it, or adjust it yourself.
explanation.commonFixesIntro: Three things that often need fixing in what AI writes:
explanation.fix1: Prices or currencies that aren't yours — sometimes it writes in dollars when you sell in pesos, soles, or Colombian pesos.
explanation.fix2: Cultural references that don't fit — "Black Friday" or "Spring Break" promotions in markets where nobody celebrates them that way.
explanation.fix3: Exaggerated promises you wouldn't make — discounts you didn't give, guarantees you don't offer, "the best" or "the only" when you wouldn't say that yourself.
explanation.conclusion: AI saves you from the blank-page block. The review is yours.

activity.title: Find what you'd change
activity.intro: Sofía asked AI for a message to invite her regulars to come on a Tuesday morning with 20% off. AI replied with this:
activity.aiExample: "Dear customer, we are pleased to offer you the best hair salon service in the country. This week we have an exclusive promotion with 50% off all our services. Don't miss this unique opportunity! 📊"
activity.instruction: Mark the problems you see (you can mark several):

issues.tooFormal: 🚫 "Dear customer" — too formal, Sofía talks like a friend
issues.exaggeratedPromise: 🚫 "the best in the country" — exaggerated promise she wouldn't make

activity.correctedExample: "Hi! 💕 This week I have open slots on Tuesday mornings and I'd love to see you. 20% off if you come that day. Want me to save you one? 😊"

quiz.title: Check what you learned
quiz.question: AI gives you a message that's technically well-written but sounds generic, like any business. What do you do?
quiz.optionA: Post it as is — AI writes better than I do.
quiz.optionB: Edit it, replacing generic phrases with words and references I actually use.
quiz.optionC: Throw it out and write it from scratch myself.
quiz.correct: Exactly! AI gave you a starting point — that already saved you time. Editing it in your voice is faster than starting from scratch, and much better than publishing something that doesn't sound like you.
quiz.incorrectExplanation: Option B combines the best of both worlds. C also works, but you lose the head start AI gave you. A is the most common mistake: publishing without a filter creates marketing that doesn't connect with your customers.

ethics.title: Moment of reflection
ethics.text: Not everything in marketing goes through AI. There are moments where writing it yourself — without help — communicates something no AI can: your real presence, your story, your care for your customers. A condolence message to a loyal customer, a personal reply to a complaint, a sincere thank-you after an important sale. Those you write yourself.
ethics.question: When in your business would you NOT use AI, even if it saves you time?
ethics.shareText: When in your business would you NOT use AI for marketing, even if it saves you time? 🤔

culminating.title: Your marketing piece, ready for today
culminating.intro: Let's bring it all together. Here you're going to produce a real message you can publish or send today.
culminating.recapEmpty: You haven't filled this in yet — go back to the previous section.
culminating.pasteLabel: Paste the response ChatGPT or Claude gave you here:
culminating.pastePlaceholder: Paste the text the AI gave you...
culminating.checksTitle: Run it through the three questions:
culminating.checkSpeaksToClient: Would I really talk to my customer this way?
culminating.fillEdited: Edit or write your final text before copying or sending it

step0.title: Prompt Writing for Women Entrepreneurs
step0.subtitle: Learn to ask AI exactly what you need for your business
step0.welcome1: Writing good prompts (instructions for AI) is a skill anyone can practice.
step0.welcome2: You don't need to be technical or an expert. You just need to know what you want and how to ask for it.
step0.welcome3: In this lesson you'll learn a simple method for talking to AI and getting useful responses for your business.
step0.encouragement: If you can write a WhatsApp message, you can write a good prompt.

step1.title: What You'll Learn
step1.objective1: I can recognize what makes a prompt effective
step1.objective2: I can write a clear prompt for a business task
step1.objective3: I can review AI output and improve my prompt if needed
step1.note: There are no wrong answers here. This is practice, and every attempt makes you better.

step2.title: Why Do Prompts Matter?
step2.text1: AI can't read your mind. It can only work with what you give it.
step2.rule: Vague prompt = vague output. Clear prompt = more useful output.
step2.example1Result: AI gives you generic ideas that don't fit your specific business.
step2.example2: I run a small candle business for busy moms. Give me 5 Instagram post ideas for spring promotions in a friendly and encouraging tone.
step2.example2Result: AI gives you ideas specific to YOUR business, YOUR audience, and YOUR style.
step2.takeaway: The difference isn't magic — it's clarity. And that's exactly what you'll learn.

step3.title: The CTO Method: Your Formula for Good Prompts
step3.intro: CTO stands for three simple things to include in every prompt:

context.title: Context
context.description: What AI needs to know about you and your situation
context.example: "I have a small bakery in my neighborhood. I sell via WhatsApp and in person."

task.title: Task
task.description: What you want the AI to do for you
task.example: "I need 3 messages to invite my customers to try my new yuca bread."

output.title: Output
output.description: How you want the response to look
output.example: "Keep them short (max 2 lines), with emojis, in a warm and friendly tone."

step3.tip: You don't have to memorize this. With practice, it becomes natural.

step4.title: Weak Prompt vs. Strong Prompt
step4.intro: Let's see real examples side by side:

example1.weak: Write me something to sell my products.
example1.strong: I have a handmade natural soap business. Write me 3 short texts for my WhatsApp status inviting customers to buy soaps as birthday gifts. Use a friendly tone with emojis.
example1.why1: It says what kind of business it is (context)
example1.why3: It says how the response should look (output)

example2.weak: Give me names for my business.
example2.strong: I'm starting a healthy desserts business for offices in the city. I need 5 creative brand names. They should sound fresh, modern, and easy to remember.
example2.why1: It explains the business type and audience
example2.why2: It asks for a specific quantity

step5.title: Guided Example: From Weak to Strong
step5.intro: Let's see how to transform a prompt step by step.
step5.original: Give me ideas for my business.
step5.problem: This prompt is too vague. AI doesn't know what kind of business you have, what you need, or how you want the response.
step5.contextText: "I have a home cleaning business. I serve families in my neighborhood."
step5.taskText: "Give me 5 Facebook post ideas to get new customers."
step5.outputText: "Make them friendly, with emojis, and include a call to action."
step5.revised:
I have a home cleaning business. I serve families in my neighborhood.

Give me 5 Facebook post ideas to get new customers.

Make them friendly, with emojis, and include a call to action.
step5.sampleResponse1: "✨🏠 Does your house need a break? We've got you covered! Deep cleaning with family-safe products. Message us today for 10% off your first cleaning. 💚"
step5.sampleResponse2: "📸 Before and after one of our cleanings. Want to see the difference in your home? Book your appointment! 🧹✨"
step5.reflection1: The response is specific to a cleaning business
step5.reflection2: The tone is friendly, as requested
step5.reflectionHuman: But remember: you decide if the response works for YOUR business. AI suggests, you decide.

step6.title: Your Turn: Write Your Own Prompt
step6.intro: Now it's your turn to practice. Use the CTO method to create your own prompt.
step6.contextLabel: Context: What does your business do? Who do you sell to?
step6.contextPlaceholder: Example: I have a decorated cookies business. I sell for parties and events...
step6.taskLabel: Task: What do you need AI to do?
step6.taskPlaceholder: Example: I need 3 promotion ideas for Mother's Day...
step6.outputLabel: Output: How do you want the response?
step6.outputPlaceholder: Example: Keep them short, with emojis, in a cheerful tone...
step6.resultLabel: Your prompt using the CTO method:
step6.hint: Copy this prompt and paste it in ChatGPT or Claude to see what AI responds.
step6.fillAll: Please fill in all three fields to generate your prompt

step7.title: Don't Trust AI Blindly
step7.intro: AI is a very useful tool, but it's not perfect. You should always review what it gives you.
step7.question1Text: AI can make up facts or give incorrect information. Verify important details.
step7.question2Text: You know your customers better than AI does. Would this content speak to them?
step7.question3Title: Does it sound like my brand?
step7.question3Text: Your business has its own voice. If the response doesn't sound like you, change it.
step7.question4Text: Always ask yourself what you'd improve. Editing AI output is part of the process.
step7.summary: AI is your assistant, not your boss. You have the final word.

step8.title: Check What You Learned
step8.intro: Answer these quick questions. Don't worry — there's no grade.

q1.question: What are characteristics of a good prompt? (choose all that apply)
q1.option1: It's short and vague so AI has freedom
q1.option2: It includes context about your business
q1.option3: It describes the task you want done
q1.option4: It explains how you want the response
q1.feedback_correct: Exactly! A good prompt includes context, task, and how you want the response.
q1.feedback_incorrect: A vague prompt gives vague results. A good prompt includes context, task, and response format.

q2.question: Which of these is a stronger prompt?
q2.option2: "I have a youth clothing store on Instagram. Give me 5 short Reel ideas to promote my new summer collection. Make them fun with trending music."
q2.feedback_correct: Correct! The second prompt has context, specific task, and describes the desired output.
q2.feedback_incorrect: The second one is stronger because it includes context (youth clothing store), task (5 Reel ideas), and output (fun, trending music).

q3.question: After receiving an AI response, what should you do?
q3.option2: Review it, verify it's correct, and edit it to match your style
q3.feedback_correct: Exactly! Always review, verify, and personalize. AI suggests, you decide.
q3.feedback_incorrect: The correct answer is to review and edit. Never publish without reviewing first.

step8.reflectionQuestion: What's one thing you would change about how you use AI after this lesson?

step9.title: Quick Reference: The CTO Method
step9.subtitle: Save this for later
step9.formulaC: CONTEXT — Tell about yourself and your business
step9.formulaO: OUTPUT — Describe how you want the response
step9.tip1: Be specific: more details = better results
step9.tip2: Include your audience: who do you sell to?
step9.tip3: Request a tone: friendly, professional, fun...
step9.tip4: Request a quantity: "give me 3 ideas" is better than "give me ideas"
step9.tip5: Always review: AI is your assistant, not your replacement
step9.examplePrompt:
I have a healthy desserts business for offices.

I need 3 texts for my WhatsApp status announcing a new weekly menu.

Keep them short, with emojis, in a fresh and professional tone.
step9.closingText1: Writing good prompts is like any skill — it improves with practice.
step9.closingText2: You've taken the first step. Now open ChatGPT or Claude, use your CTO method, and start experimenting.
step9.closingText3: You are capable of using AI smartly and creatively for your business.

successCriteria.introTitle: By the end of this module you will be able to:

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

dashboard.subheading: Pick up your learning whenever you'd like.
